title: ESL — Natural Daily Conversations
description: Common phrases and sentences used in everyday American English. Great for building natural rhythm.
sentences: Hey, how's it going?
Not bad, thanks. How about yourself?
Pretty good. Staying busy, you know how it is.
Tell me about it. I've been swamped at work lately.
What have you been up to?
Oh, you know, same old same old.
I've been meaning to call you — we should catch up sometime.
Definitely. Let me know when you're free.
Are you doing anything this weekend?
I might have some plans, but nothing set in stone.
We should grab coffee or something.
That sounds great. I'm down for that.
I'll shoot you a text later and we can figure something out.
Works for me. Looking forward to it.
Sorry, I don't mean to cut this short, but I gotta run.
No worries. It was good running into you.
Take care. Talk to you soon.
For sure. See you around.
I was just about to head out — do you want to walk together?
Sure, that works. I'm going that way anyway.
Did you hear about what happened at the meeting?
No, I missed it. What went down?
You're not going to believe this.
Come on, just tell me. Don't leave me hanging.
Okay, so apparently they're changing the whole process.
Wait, seriously? How do you feel about that?
Honestly? I'm still trying to wrap my head around it.
I get that. It's a lot to take in.
I guess we'll just have to wait and see how it plays out.
Yeah, no point stressing about things we can't control.
